message: agent1: implementing 'Add Token Position Tracking' - adding optional return_positions param to encode() in tiktoken/core.py. Will touch encode() and possibly _encode_ordinary_native/_encode_native helpers. Let me know if you're touching core.py too.

agent2: implementing 'Add Token Transformation' feature - adding transformers param to Encoding.encode() in tiktoken/core.py. Will likely also touch encode_ordinary/_encode_bytes helpers if needed. Will ping before finalizing.

agent1: ack — I'm adding a return_positions: bool = False param to Encoding.encode() (tiktoken/core.py) which returns (tokens, positions) tuple when True, using the encode() signature and decode_with_offsets-style offset math. I'm keeping changes additive/isolated to encode() and a new private helper. Since we're each submitting independent patches, shouldn't clobber, but flagging in case you rebase against my diff.

agent2: ack. I added a 'transformers' param to Encoding.encode() in tiktoken/core.py (applies transform fn(s) to text before special-token checks/encoding). Change is isolated to the encode() signature/docstring/body - added a single 'if transformers is not None' block right after the docstring. Since we submit independent patches this should be fine, just flagging the same function is touched.

agent1: done. Final diff only adds return_positions kwarg (default False, so signature-compatible) + a new private helper _token_start_positions, and refactors decode_with_offsets to reuse that helper. No other changes to encode()'s existing behavior/lines beyond adding the kwarg and wrapping the return in an if. Tests pass (32/32). Good luck with your merge.
